summary: Strong JD-aligned content but contains multiple fabricated metrics and unsupported claims that violate the source-of-truth brief, plus a hallucinated 'Gerrit' tool.

issues.issue: Fabricated release-cycle improvement. The brief explicitly forbids claiming the 82% / 45→8 min reduction — that improvement predates Uday; he may only claim ~8-minute deploys as a sustained state.
issues.where: Summary: 'delivering **82% faster release cycles**' and Experience bullet: 'cutting release cycles by **82% (45 → 8 min)**'
issues.suggestion: Replace with 'sustaining fast, automated (~8-minute) deployments' — remove the 82% and 45→8 framing entirely.

issues.issue: Fabricated provisioning-speed and drift metrics. Brief states 'do NOT claim 80% faster / 95% drift reduction' — original build, not his improvement.
issues.where: Summary: '**80% faster provisioning**' and Experience bullet: 'reducing provisioning time **80%** and configuration drift **95%**'
issues.suggestion: Replace with 'delivering consistent, reproducible provisioning across Dev, Staging, and Production' — drop the 80% and 95%.

issues.issue: Hallucinated tool 'Gerrit' — not in the candidate's inventory anywhere. The master bullet says 'Git code-review workflows' only.
issues.where: Experience bullet: 'integrated SonarQube quality gates and Git/Gerrit code-review workflows into CI/CD'
issues.suggestion: Remove 'Gerrit'; use 'Git code-review workflows'. Also consider dropping SonarQube since JD doesn't ask for it (Tier 2).

issues.issue: Healthcare prototype production-scale metrics used despite explicit warning. Brief flags '500+ concurrent users,' '5+ overly permissive IAM roles removed,' and '~30% image pull latency reduction' as hard-to-defend prototype claims to keep out by default.
issues.where: Healthcare project bullets: 'removed **5+ overly permissive roles**' and '**~30% reduced image pull latency** while serving **500+ concurrent users**'
issues.suggestion: Reframe to prototype scale: describe dual-registry GHCR/ECR strategy and least-privilege IAM without the 500+ users, 5+ roles, or 30% latency numbers.

issues.issue: 'Architected' used for existing/maintained systems, violating the honesty rule to avoid platform-origination verbs.
issues.where: Salon project: 'Architected serverless build/deploy system'; Summary/CRM also use 'Designed multi-environment AWS architecture'
issues.suggestion: Use 'Maintained and extended' (Salon) and 'Operated and extended' (CRM) per the brief's approved verbs.

issues.issue: Tier 2 tools surfaced despite JD not requiring them and skills section correctly omitting OpenShift/Istio/ELK/Datadog — creating internal inconsistency and overstating depth.
issues.where: Experience bullet 3: 'Kubernetes (EKS, OpenShift)' with 'Istio service mesh'; bullet 4: 'ELK Stack, and Datadog'
issues.suggestion: Remove OpenShift, Istio, ELK Stack, and Datadog from bullets to match the skills section and JD (JD lists Istio only as nice-to-have; keep it out or add to skills if surfaced).

issues.issue: Skills section omits Istio, OpenShift, ELK Stack, Datadog, Ansible, Azure, KMS, TLS/SSL, Branching Strategies (present in original cv.md) but experience bullets still reference OpenShift/Istio/ELK/Datadog — skills and bullets disagree.
issues.where: Skills 'Containers & Kubernetes' row vs. Experience bullet 3 'Istio service mesh'
issues.suggestion: Align: whatever tools appear in bullets should appear in skills, and vice versa. Given JD, remove the Tier 2 tools from both.

issues.issue: colorScheme is 'teal' (startup/SaaS) but format rules set Navy as DEFAULT for Cloud/SRE/enterprise; a Senior DevOps role at an enterprise-sounding company arguably warrants navy. Minor, judgment call.
issues.suggestion: Consider navy per default guidance unless targeting a startup; low priority.

issues.issue: JD lists Istio and GitOps as nice-to-have; GitOps is covered (ArgoCD) but Istio is dropped from skills. If Istio kept in a bullet it should also be an ATS-scannable skill.
issues.where: Skills section — no 'Istio' or 'GitOps' keyword row
issues.suggestion: Add 'ArgoCD (GitOps)' emphasis is present; optionally add Istio to skills only if it stays in a bullet. Otherwise fine — core required keywords (Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, AWS EKS/EC2/S3, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Prometheus, Grafana, Python, Linux, IaC) are all well covered.

issues.issue: Title targets 'Senior DevOps Engineer' but candidate is 1+ year (Junior-Mid per profile). Not a resume-content error, but seniority mismatch worth noting.
issues.where: JD 'Senior DevOps Engineer' vs. Summary '1+ year'
issues.suggestion: No fabrication needed; keep honest '1+ year'. Awareness only.

issues.issue: Once fabricated metrics are removed, several bullets lose their quantified punch (release cycle, provisioning). Ensure remaining real metrics (99.9% uptime, 70% toil, 30% cost, 60%+ build time, RPO<1h) are surfaced prominently.
issues.where: Experience bullets 1 and 2 after metric removal
issues.suggestion: Lead bullet 1 with '~8-minute automated deploys across 20+ microservices'; keep 99.9% uptime and 70% toil as the quantified anchors.

issues.issue: Contact info (name, email, phone, location, LinkedIn, GitHub), summary, skills, experience with titles+dates, education, and achievements are all present and complete.
No spelling errors or typos found; British -ise spelling ('Dockerised') applied consistently per format rules.
Grammar, bullet punctuation (all end with periods), and parallel action-verb structure are consistent and correct.
No placeholder text, no first-person pronouns, no duplicate bullets, no lorem/TODO/XYZ found. (Truthfulness fabrications are logged separately above.)
issues.suggestion: No action — no placeholder/pronoun red flags.
